narration: Every price tag ending in nine was put there on purpose. That $9.99 isn't saving you a penny — it's a psychological weapon costing you $1,400 a year.
visual: Dark background. Large price tag SVG showing $9.99 with a red strikethrough animating across it. Text below: 'Your brain says: ≈ $9'. Title CHARM PRICING fades in amber.

narration: Here's the trick: your brain reads left to right and anchors on the first digit. So $9.99 feels way closer to nine dollars than to ten — even though it's almost ten.
visual: Light background. Brain SVG. Number '9' springs in large and highlighted in amber. Arrow pointing left labeled 'anchors here'. The '.99' fades out in gray.

narration: Sixty percent of all retail prices end in nine — not by accident. You run into over two hundred of these charm prices every grocery trip, and each one nudges you to buy.
visual: Dark background. Nine animated price tags spring in: $1.99, $4.99, $9.99, $14.99, $19.99, $29.99, $39.99, $49.99, $99.99. Counter animates to 60%.

narration: MIT tested this with the same dress sold at $34 and $39. The $39 version outsold $34 by twenty-four percent — a higher price sold more, all because of the nine.
visual: Light background. Two dress SVGs side by side. Left: gray dress labeled $34. Right: amber dress labeled $39 with a checkmark. Bar chart grows: $39 bar towers 24% taller.

narration: Across groceries, clothing, and subscriptions, charm pricing costs the average American $1,400 every year. Over forty years, that's $56,000 — gone because of a one-cent trick.
visual: Dark background. Piggy bank SVG. Dollar counter animates from $0 to $1,400 with amber glow. Then '$56,000 over 40 years' springs in red below.

narration: Your defense: mentally round every price up to the next dollar. $9.99 is ten dollars. That two-second habit can save you $1,400 a year — follow for more tricks like this.
visual: Light background. Shield SVG with checkmark. Two steps spring in: 1) Round UP — $9.99 = $10. 2) Ask 'Would I buy it at $10?' CTA: follow for money psychology.
